headline: Field notes from the seat.
note: Markdown-first. No takes, just the patterns I'm actually shipping with.

posts.title: Teams Rarely Behave Irrationally. Systems Do.
posts.excerpt: Why my first move on a struggling team is to distrust the obvious explanation — and look for the one constraint that explains the most symptoms.

posts.title: Most Orgs Don't Need Better Estimates. They Need Forecasting.
posts.excerpt: The case for probabilistic, percentile-based forecasting over deterministic estimation — and why it matches how delivery systems actually behave.

posts.title: AI Is Where Agile Was Fifteen Years Ago.
posts.excerpt: Tools bought, a few teams experimenting, no operating model. The hard part was never the tooling — it's organizational adoption.
